game_name: Populous II: Trials of the Olympian Gods

danger_signals.visual_form: Colossus, Medusa, and other mythological creatures wandering the map
danger_signals.meaning: Critical world state: one side has achieved territorial dominance (75%+ map control); urgent warning to accelerate victory before indestructible entities devastate both factions indiscriminately

danger_signals.visual_form: Shape-shifting fungal growth on terrain following cellular automata patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard and divine punishment; area denial mechanism that kills any entity stepping on infected ground

danger_signals.visual_form: Manna meter at critical low levels
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of divine power availability; faction weakness and inability to deploy intervention effects

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy hero unit (one of six legendary Greek mythological figures) wandering map
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent has activated category-specific heroic power; direct threat requiring immediate counter-strategy

safety_signals.visual_form: Increased number of follower units and expanding settlements
safety_signals.meaning: Faction strength and stability; higher population generates more manna per time unit, enabling sustained divine intervention

safety_signals.visual_form: Religious structures built by followers, including in contested/enemy regions
safety_signals.meaning: Territorial claim, faith consolidation, and recovery mechanism; allows losing factions to rebuild influence even under pressure
Divine power availability; faction readiness to deploy major intervention effects across six categories (earth, water, wind, fire, plants, people)

safety_signals.visual_form: Experience points awarded (1-5 scale) after map completion
safety_signals.meaning: Long-term faction empowerment; points boost power accuracy, duration, and reduce manna costs for future engagements

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Greek mythological hero units (category-specific legendary figures)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled settlements, temples, and terrain alterations; manna generation tied to player population density

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Enemy hero unit manifestation (one of six Greek mythological figures per category)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy followers and structures visually distinct from player faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy-controlled territory, enemy settlements, enemy temples; enemy population drives opposing manna generation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Indestructible entities that attack both factions indiscriminately
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fungal hazards; celestial/mythological visual effects tied to divine intervention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Both factions occupying roughly equal map portions; multiple settlement clusters visible; lower density of catastrophic events
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Followers freely moving and expanding territory; both sides capable of sustained manna generation and power deployment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: One faction's territory visibly expanding; enemy settlements shrinking; increased divine intervention effect visibility
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dominant faction deploying more frequent powers; losing faction transitioning to defensive temple-building strategy; manna usage becomes asymmetric

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ancient monsters materialize and begin traversing the map; trail of destruction visible behind monster movement; urgent red/warning visual cues
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All units (player, enemy, monsters) become targets for destruction; monsters ignore faction affiliation; pressure to achieve victory escalates dramatically

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temples appearing across enemy territory as visual markers of faith persistence; settlement density visibly lower; manna meter depleted
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Followers prioritize temple construction even in hostile territory; reduced power deployment; defensive posturing; slower territorial expansion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Complete territorial control of current map; all enemy structures eliminated; celebration or transition visual; experience points displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player advances multiple maps (map skipping mechanic); progression to next deity opponent; experience-boosted powers applied to next engagement

environmental_storytelling.example: Player sequentially battles different Greek deities (Aphrodite, Dionysus, others), culminating in Zeus; each opponent represents escalating divine challenge and mythological narrative
Deadly fungus with cellular automata growth patterns visualizes supernatural plague; shape-shifting visual behavior communicates that this is not natural phenomena but divine punishment
Visual density and organization of follower settlements indicates faction health, morale, and likelihood of recovery; sparse settlements = faction weakness

environmental_storytelling.technique: Monster manifestation as narrative climax trigger
environmental_storytelling.example: Unleashed ancient monsters serve dual purpose: gameplay escalation mechanic and mythological storytelling (gods releasing legendary creatures upon mortals)
Temples appearing in enemy territory visually communicate stubborn faith and resistance; tells story of followers maintaining belief despite territorial loss

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Actively spreading into new territory, constructing settlements and temples, visible manna generation
Clustering around existing temples, prioritizing defensive temple construction over territorial expansion
Wandering map attacking followers or performing 'nefarious acts'; indicates opponent has activated category power and is exerting direct force
Indiscriminate movement across entire map, leaving trail of destruction, attacking both player and enemy followers; signals game state urgency
Capable of deploying multiple divine interventions across six categories; active engagement with map manipulation
Limited divine intervention capability; reliant on passive manna generation and population growth; forced to wait and defend

notes: Populous II communicates world-state primarily through: (1) territory control visualization and density, (2) manna meter as faction power indicator, (3) monster manifestation as critical state trigger, (4) temple placement as persistent faction presence marker, (5) mythological theming as narrative scaffolding. The game uses asymmetric visual communication: players see their own faction state clearly while enemy state is inferred from territorial control, monster behavior, and opponent hero deployments. The 75% threshold mechanic creates a clear danger signal through monster spawning—a visual and behavioral cascade that reframes the entire game state as urgent. Tier T2 qualitative data: sources describe visual mechanics and behavioral patterns but lack granular timing/coordinate data.
